qa_pairs.question: How does one mark changes made to an existing wiki page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: To mark changes on an existing wiki page for translation, first, ensure that any new text intended for translation is enclosed within `<translate>` tags. Conversely, exclude code blocks and wiki markup (like tables and other tags) from translation by enclosing them within `</translate><translate>` tags. Next, go into "View" mode on the page. You should see a message stating, "This page has changes since it was last marked for translation." Click on the "marked for translation" link provided. Review the generated translation units to confirm that code blocks and wiki markup are excluded, and all plain text is included. In the "Priority languages" section, specify either "fr" or "en" as the language into which the page needs to be translated. Finally, click "Mark this version for translation." If a change is minor, such as a typo, and doesn't require adjustments to other-language versions, you can select the "Do not invalidate translations" checkbox on the "Page translation" page.

qa_pairs.question: Why are code blocks not translated on the wiki, and what are the recommendations for handling comments within them?
qa_pairs.answer: Code blocks are not translated on the wiki because the professional human translators are not programmers and cannot differentiate between code and comments across various programming languages. The documentation team has instructed them to exclude code blocks from translation. To ensure that information from comments is still accessible to users reading translated pages, contributors should consider these suggestions: move the information from comments outside the code block into the surrounding text, which will be translated; leave an index comment (e.g., "NOTE 1") to connect the external explanatory text to the relevant line of code; or, if sufficiently bilingual and familiar with the translation tools, translate the code block themselves. It is important not to leave example code uncommented, but contributors should remember that comments will generally not be translated and consider how this affects a translated page's understanding.
